channel.title: World
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from World, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Nose for trouble: Italian town seeks ‘odour evaluators’ to sniff out bad smells
item.description: Mayor of Brendola in Vicenza says he has received complaints from residents who live near industrial zones An Italian town is seeking a crew of sniffe...

item.title: Rubio tells Europe US wants renewed alliance – but on Trump’s terms
item.description: Secretary of state calls the US ‘a child of Europe’ and urges continent to back a new world order Munich security conference live: Europe must be read...

item.title: US man who fled jail and pleaded to Trump and Kim Kardashian gets 60-year term
item.description: Antoine Massey was convicted on charges of rape and kidnapping before New Orleans jailbreak A man who joined nine others in fleeing a New Orleans jail...

item.title: Munich Security Conference live: Europe must be ready to fight Russia as ‘warning signs are there’, says Starmer
item.description: Russia has shown ‘appetite for aggression’ and possible path for Europe’s future, UK prime minister says Rubio insists that the US “do not seek to sep...

item.title: Peter Attia appears to be staying at CBS News despite Epstein communications
item.description: Staffers believe network has decided to retain Attia, who issued apology after inappropriate Epstein emails, as on-air analyst Two weeks after a trove...

item.title: 'It all kicked off' Canada and Sweden curlers argue over 'double-tapping'
item.description: Sweden's Oskar Eriksson and Canada's Marc Kennedy exchange tense words after the Swedes accused the Canadian's of double-tapping their stones during t...

item.title: GB women shock curling world champions Canada
item.description: Great Britain's women curlers kickstart their campaign with a superb first victory of the 2026 Winter Olympics, beating medal contenders Canada 7-6.

item.title: 'I told him where to stick it' - Canada and Sweden in curling row
item.description: When the men's rinks of Canada and Sweden met at the Winter Olympics in Cortina on Friday, tempers became frayed.

item.title: Guimaraes set to be out for two months with injury
item.description: Newcastle United captain Bruno Guimaraes is expected to be out for a couple of months after sustaining a hamstring injury.

item.title: William and Catherine all smiles in Valentine's Day photograph
item.description: The previously unseen photo was shared with the caption "Happy Valentine's Day" along with a heart emoji.
